main_question_answer: No. A reviewer-defensible L1 comparison under close-enough backbone plus explicit realized-length accounting is not currently supportable on the canonical matched surface without substantial additional work and official artifacts.

released_checkpoints_or_official_code_us.current_repo_support: MODE A inference-only adapter; MODE B import-validation only when externally produced official artifacts are supplied.

backbone_match_analysis.risk: MODE A is not official L1 policy; MODE B may use different RL-trained model family and is marked non-apples-to-apples.

fairness_contract_audit.details: Canonical matched-surface raw_case_results has no realized token fields; MODE A reporting uses generated_tokens_estimate and does not separate realized reasoning vs final answer tokens.

reviewer_defensibility_assessment: easily attacked if promoted as next major external strengthening now
single_biggest_blocker: Missing canonical-surface realized-length accounting granularity (requested budget vs realized reasoning tokens vs final answer tokens vs total) coupled with lack of integrated official L1 checkpoint evaluation path.
exact_next_recommendation: Stop here for now: keep L1 as discussion/appendix context only, do not prioritize full strengthening before a dedicated token-accounting schema upgrade and verified official artifact import package are available.
